steps.step: Write the perimeter formula for a rectangle: $P = 2(\text{length} + \text{width})$. Substitute length $= x+35$ and width $= x$: $P = 2((x+35)+x) = 2(2x+35) = 4x+70$.
The problem states the perimeter is *at least* 520 centimeters. "At least" means greater than or equal to, so the inequality is $4x + 70 \geq 520$.

• Choice A: $2x + 35 \leq 520$. The left side uses only one copy of each term (not doubled for both sides) and the direction is wrong ($\leq$ instead of $\geq$). Eliminated.
  • Choice B: $2x + 35 \geq 520$. The inequality direction is correct, but $2x+35$ is not the perimeter — it is only half of $4x+70$. Eliminated.
  • Choice C: $4x + 70 \leq 520$. The left side is the correct perimeter expression, but $\leq$ means "at most," opposite of the problem's "at least." Eliminated.
  • Choice D: $4x + 70 \geq 520$. Correct perimeter $4x+70$ and correct direction $\geq$ for "at least." That matches choice D — D is correct.
